insight: In the 2026 World Cup, Bosnia-Herzegovina's performance after hydration breaks has been somewhat inconsistent, with the team gaining momentum in only half of the breaks they've had, resulting in a slight average decline in momentum of 0.100 per minute. When they were pressing their opponents before a break, they tended to lose momentum afterwards, with an average decline of 0.200 per minute and failing to maintain their momentum in any of these instances. However, when they weren't pressing before a break, they were able to stabilize their performance, with no average change in momentum, suggesting that the team may benefit from a more conservative approach leading up to hydration breaks. Overall, these statistics suggest that Bosnia-Herzegovina's strategy and performance leading up to hydration breaks can have a significant impact on their ability to maintain or gain momentum in a match.
